title: Issue when trying to deploy a lambda function with serverless ecr support
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

When trying to deploy a lambda function to aws lambda with serverless, using an [ECR image](https://www.serverless.com/blog/container-support-for-lambda), the deployment fails with the error message 
```
✖ Stack metadata-tagger-dev failed to deploy (262s)

Error:                                                                                                                 
CREATE_FAILED: ExampleLambdaFunction (AWS::Lambda::Function)                                                 
Resource handler returned message: "Source image xxx.dkr.ecr.eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/serverless-function-dev@sha256:{some_sha} does not exist. Provide a valid source
 image. (Service: Lambda, Status Code: 400, Request ID: xx)" (RequestToken: xxx, HandlerErrorCode: InvalidRequest)
```
When checking whether the image `xxx.dkr.ecr.eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/serverless-function-dev@sha256:{some_sha}` exists, it turns out, that an image with this sha does indeed not exists.
There exists however another image in the same repository with a different sha.

When trying to create the function with docker, the deployment runs successfully
<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create a serverless function running in an (ECR) container image

2. deploy with `sls deploy`

**Describe the results you received:**
The deployment fails with the above described error message

**Describe the results you expected:**
The function gets deployed successfully

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.1.0
API Version:  4.1.0
Go Version:   go1.18
Built:        Fri May  6 18:15:54 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body: Can you provide a simple reproducer with podman commands or curl commands against the api endpoints?
I did not exactly understand what you meant, but I created a minimum working example [here](https://github.com/AntonOellerer/sls-podman-ecr-triage)

I have zero experience with aws labda.
We need a reproducer with podman commands or since this is likely using the api the exact api commands which where used.
If you start the podman service in debug mode it should output the exact api requests.

Ok, then I will try that tomorrow
How do I start the service in debug mode?
I am also using `podman-docker` v 4.1.0 btw
`podman --log-level debug system service -t0` will start the service

It seems as if the api is not used, since I do not get any useful log output
I found the code doing the image creation and ecr-pushing [here](https://github.com/serverless/serverless/blob/ed111e021b286eb4a68f9b2fce7fb641e03340bd/lib/plugins/aws/provider.js#L2101)
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Is this still an open issue?
I am currently working on other projects, if I come around to verify it, I will notify you
Hey, I had to work with it again and it seems as if it was fixed, thank you!
Ok, it seems as if the issue still persists, sorry
